description: This set of micrographs presents the microstructure of Fe-Mn-Al-C low-density steels (LDSs) after different processing routes, with a uniform 30 μm scale bar for each subfigure. Subfigure (a) corresponds to sample S0 (hot rolled and water quenched without solution treatment), showing non-uniform grains containing deformed structures, nanoscale κ-carbides and high dislocation density. Subfigure (b) displays sample S950 (solution treated at 950 ℃ for 2 h then water quenched), featuring fine equiaxed face-centered cubic austenite grains with average size of 8.7 μm and high density of annealing twin boundaries. Subfigure (c) shows sample S1050 (solution treated at 1050 ℃ for 2 h) with medium-sized equiaxed austenite grains. Subfigure (d) presents sample S1150 (solution treated at 1150 ℃ for 2h), with coarse equiaxed austenite grains of 32 μm average size and low twin boundary density. The micrographs verify that LDS grain size increases with rising solution temperature, supporting the core conclusion that grain size optimization via solution treatment regulates oxide film formation and corrosion resistance of LDSs.
